lines.english: Lesson 32
lines.chinese: 第32课
lines.raw: Lesson 32 | 第32课

lines.english: A lost ship
lines.chinese: 一艘失踪的船
lines.raw: A lost ship | 一艘失踪的船

lines.english: Did the crew of the Elkor find what they were looking for? Why?
lines.chinese: “埃尔科”号的全体船员找到他们要寻找的东西了吗？为什么？
lines.raw: Did the crew of the Elkor find what they were looking for? Why? | “埃尔科”号的全体船员找到他们要寻找的东西了吗？为什么？

lines.english: The salvage operation had been a complete failure.
lines.chinese: 打捞行动彻底失败了。
lines.raw: The salvage operation had been a complete failure. | 打捞行动彻底失败了。

lines.english: The small ship, Elkor, which had been searching the Barents Sea for weeks, was on its way home.
lines.chinese: “埃尔科”号这艘小船在巴伦支海域搜寻了几个星期后，正在返航的途中。
lines.raw: The small ship, Elkor, which had been searching the Barents Sea for weeks, was on its way home. | “埃尔科”号这艘小船在巴伦支海域搜寻了几个星期后，正在返航的途中。

lines.english: A radio message from the mainland had been received by the ship's captain instructing him to give up the search.
lines.chinese: 船长收到了一份来自大陆的电报，指示他放弃搜寻。
lines.raw: A radio message from the mainland had been received by the ship's captain instructing him to give up the search. | 船长收到了一份来自大陆的电报，指示他放弃搜寻。

lines.english: The captain knew that another attempt would be made later,
lines.chinese: 船长知道以后还会再进行一次尝试，
lines.raw: The captain knew that another attempt would be made later, | 船长知道以后还会再进行一次尝试，

lines.english: for the sunken ship he was trying to find had been carrying a precious cargo of gold bullion.
lines.chinese: 因为他试图寻找的那艘沉船载有一批珍贵的金条。
lines.raw: for the sunken ship he was trying to find had been carrying a precious cargo of gold bullion. | 因为他试图寻找的那艘沉船载有一批珍贵的金条。

lines.english: Despite the message, the captain of the Elkor decided to try once more.
lines.chinese: 尽管收到那份电报，“埃尔科”号的船长还是决定再试一次。
lines.raw: Despite the message, the captain of the Elkor decided to try once more. | 尽管收到那份电报，“埃尔科”号的船长还是决定再试一次。

lines.english: The sea bed was scoured with powerful nets and there was tremendous excitement on board when a chest was raised from the bottom.
lines.chinese: 海底被强力拖网仔细搜寻，当一个箱子从海底被捞上来时，船上的人都非常兴奋。
lines.raw: The sea bed was scoured with powerful nets and there was tremendous excitement on board when a chest was raised from the bottom. | 海底被强力拖网仔细搜寻，当一个箱子从海底被捞上来时，船上的人都非常兴奋。

lines.english: Though the crew were at first under the impression that the lost ship had been found,
lines.chinese: 虽然船员们最初以为找到了那艘失踪的船，
lines.raw: Though the crew were at first under the impression that the lost ship had been found, | 虽然船员们最初以为找到了那艘失踪的船，

lines.english: the contents of the chest proved them wrong.
lines.chinese: 但箱子里的东西证明他们错了。
lines.raw: the contents of the chest proved them wrong. | 但箱子里的东西证明他们错了。

lines.english: What they had in fact found was a ship which had been sunk many years before.
lines.chinese: 事实上，他们找到的是一艘多年前就已经沉没的船。
lines.raw: What they had in fact found was a ship which had been sunk many years before. | 事实上，他们找到的是一艘多年前就已经沉没的船。

lines.english: The chest contained the personal belongings of a seaman, Alan Fielding.
lines.chinese: 箱子里装着一个名叫艾伦·菲尔丁的海员的个人物品。
lines.raw: The chest contained the personal belongings of a seaman, Alan Fielding. | 箱子里装着一个名叫艾伦·菲尔丁的海员的个人物品。

lines.english: There were books, clothing and photographs, together with letters which the seaman had once received from his wife.
lines.chinese: 里面有书、衣服和照片，还有海员以前从他妻子那里收到的信件。
lines.raw: There were books, clothing and photographs, together with letters which the seaman had once received from his wife. | 里面有书、衣服和照片，还有海员以前从他妻子那里收到的信件。

lines.english: The captain of the Elkor ordered his men to salvage as much as possible from the wreck.
lines.chinese: “埃尔科”号的船长命令他的船员尽可能多地从残骸中打捞物品。
lines.raw: The captain of the Elkor ordered his men to salvage as much as possible from the wreck. | “埃尔科”号的船长命令他的船员尽可能多地从残骸中打捞物品。

lines.english: Nothing of value was found,
lines.chinese: 没找到什么有价值的东西，
lines.raw: Nothing of value was found, | 没找到什么有价值的东西，

lines.english: but the numerous items which were brought to the surface proved to be of great interest.
lines.chinese: 但打捞上来的许多物品却引起了人们极大的兴趣。
lines.raw: but the numerous items which were brought to the surface proved to be of great interest. | 但打捞上来的许多物品却引起了人们极大的兴趣。

lines.english: From a heavy gun that was raised, the captain realized that the ship must have been a cruiser.
lines.chinese: 从打捞上来的一门重型火炮，船长意识到这艘船一定是一艘巡洋舰。
lines.raw: From a heavy gun that was raised, the captain realized that the ship must have been a cruiser. | 从打捞上来的一门重型火炮，船长意识到这艘船一定是一艘巡洋舰。

lines.english: In another chest which contained the belongings of a ship's officer,
lines.chinese: 在另一个箱子里，装着一名船上军官的物品，
lines.raw: In another chest which contained the belongings of a ship's officer, | 在另一个箱子里，装着一名船上军官的物品，

lines.english: there was an unfinished letter which had been written on March 14th, 1943.
lines.chinese: 里面有一封写于1943年3月14日、尚未写完的信。
lines.raw: there was an unfinished letter which had been written on March 14th, 1943. | 里面有一封写于1943年3月14日、尚未写完的信。

lines.english: The captain learnt from the letter that the name of the lost ship was the Karen.
lines.chinese: 船长从信中得知那艘失踪的船名叫“卡伦”号。
lines.raw: The captain learnt from the letter that the name of the lost ship was the Karen. | 船长从信中得知那艘失踪的船名叫“卡伦”号。

lines.english: The most valuable find of all was the ship's log book, parts of which it was still possible to read.
lines.chinese: 最有价值的发现是船上的航海日志，其中一部分仍然可以阅读。
lines.raw: The most valuable find of all was the ship's log book, parts of which it was still possible to read. | 最有价值的发现是船上的航海日志，其中一部分仍然可以阅读。

lines.english: From this the captain was able to piece together all the information that had come to light.
lines.chinese: 从日志中，船长得以把所有已发现的信息拼凑起来。
lines.raw: From this the captain was able to piece together all the information that had come to light. | 从日志中，船长得以把所有已发现的信息拼凑起来。

lines.english: The Karen had been sailing in a convoy to Russia when she was torpedoed by an enemy submarine.
lines.chinese: “卡伦”号当时正在驶往俄罗斯的护航船队中，结果被一艘敌方潜艇鱼雷击中。
lines.raw: The Karen had been sailing in a convoy to Russia when she was torpedoed by an enemy submarine. | “卡伦”号当时正在驶往俄罗斯的护航船队中，结果被一艘敌方潜艇鱼雷击中。

lines.english: This was later confirmed by a naval official at the Ministry of Defence after the Elkor had returned home.
lines.chinese: “埃尔科”号返回家园后，国防部的一名海军官员证实了这一点。
lines.raw: This was later confirmed by a naval official at the Ministry of Defence after the Elkor had returned home. | “埃尔科”号返回家园后，国防部的一名海军官员证实了这一点。

lines.english: All the items that were found were sent to the War Museum.
lines.chinese: 所有找到的物品都被送往战争博物馆。
lines.raw: All the items that were found were sent to the War Museum. | 所有找到的物品都被送往战争博物馆。
